summary: The one-command closure is correctly owned by run-session: the new policy owner is callback-injected, has no CLI/Gate/PR/connector/review imports, reuses the merged production connector and independent-review owners, preserves legacy/profile fallback, and cannot create or merge a PR, waive a gate, or perform an external side effect.
inspection_summary: Inspected the current-head architecture and Story, the origin/main-to-HEAD source surface, module imports and composition edges, target-model ownership/conformance evidence, predecessor closure records, human-authority boundaries, and focused regression tests for the one-command owner, independent review, and scope boundary.
inspection_inputs: rg -n "from './cli.js'" across the run-session owner surface (no matches)
judgment_delta: Initial concern: the final Story might duplicate the already-merged runtime connector or review orchestration -> Final conclusion: it adds one run-session policy owner and only the minimum objective/polling/rebind integration to the existing connector and review owners; provider, lifecycle, provenance, and verdict contracts remain in their shipped modules.

Initial concern: the one-command path might create a run-session-to-CLI reverse dependency -> Final conclusion: src/one-command-pr-ready-closure.js imports only node:timers/promises, guarded-run-session injects readiness/prepare/runtime boundaries, and the only CLI edge is the permitted outer composition root into production runtime dependencies.

Initial concern: guarded autonomy might cross human authority or break legacy Runs -> Final conclusion: the autonomous registry ends at final_prepare/pr_ready, objectives explicitly prohibit PR creation/merge/waiver/external effects, the owner rejects unknown authority seams, and explicit legacy plus disabled-autonomous fallback remain persisted and tested.

Initial concern: target architecture or a secondary output surface might regress -> Final conclusion: current-head conformance is 80 violations versus the 81-violation origin/main baseline with no new run-session-to-CLI reverse dependency, and focused tests cover new/default, legacy/fallback, review lifecycle, typed-stop, and scope-boundary surfaces.
